docs.title: wafrunner-cli
docs.text: Command-line interface for the wafrunner vulnerability research platform.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Configure your API token
wafrunner configure

# Download CVE lookup data
wafrunner update

# Create a collection of vulnerabilities to work with
wafrunner collection create my-vulns --id CVE-2021-44228 --id CVE-2024-1234

# Run research tasks
wafrunner research github -c my-vulns
wafrunner research scrape -c my-vulns
wafrunner research classify -c my-vulns
wafrunner research init-graph -c my-vulns

# Download exploit graphs locally
wafrunner data get-graph -c my-vulns

# Check status
wafrunner research show -c my-vulns

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Basic Usage — Getting started
  • Configure CLI — API token and path settings
  • Collection Commands — Managing vulnerability collections
  • Research Commands — Running research tasks
  • Data Commands — Downloading artifacts
  • Command Reference — All commands at a glance

docs.title: Command Reference
docs.text: Quick reference for all wafrunner commands.

docs.title: Top-level Commands
docs.text: Command Description wafrunner configure Set API token, forge path, and log directory wafrunner update Download latest CVE-to-vulnID lookup data wafrunner shell Enter interactive shell with tab completion

docs.title: collection — Manage Vulnerability Collections
docs.text: Command Description collection create <name> Create a new collection from --id flags or --file collection list List all local collections collection show <name> Display contents of a collection collection delete <name> Remove a collection

docs.title: research — Run Research Tasks
docs.text:
All commands accept --collection/-c or --id/-i.

Command Description research github Search GitHub for related repositories research scrape Scrape data source links research classify Run classifier on scraped data research init-graph Initialize exploit graphs (skips existing) research refine-graph Refine existing exploit graphs research init-scdef Initialize security control definitions research update-source Update vulnerability data from NIST research links View data source links and statuses research show View exploit graph, SCDEF, and link status

docs.title: data — Download Artifacts
docs.text: Command Description data get-graph Download exploit graphs to local JSON files data get-schema Fetch the exploit graph JSON schema data get-controls Download security controls (placeholder)

docs.title: test — Forge Test Execution
docs.text: Command Description test run Execute a Forge test run test status Check status of a test run test list List test runs test stop Stop a running test test logs View test run logs

docs.title: update — CVE Data
docs.text:
wafrunner update                    # Download latest CVE lookup data
wafrunner update -v                 # Show new CVEs
wafrunner update -s                 # Save new CVEs to a collection
wafrunner update -s -n my-new-cves  # Save with a custom collection name

docs.title: Testing Guide for wafrunner-cli
docs.text: This guide documents the standard testing practices for this CLI application.

docs.title: Testing Stack
docs.text:
• pytest: Primary testing framework
  • typer.testing.CliRunner: For testing CLI commands
  • flake8: Code linting
  • black: Code formatting
  • pre-commit: Git hooks for automated checks

docs.title: 1. Unit Tests
docs.text:
Test individual functions and components in isolation.

Location: tests/test_*.py Run: pytest tests/ or make test

Example:

def test_api_client_initialization():
    # Test specific functionality
    pass

docs.title: 2. CLI Command Tests
docs.text:
Test that commands are properly registered and work correctly.

Location: tests/test_*_commands.py Run: pytest tests/ -k "commands" or make test-cli

Example:

from typer.testing import CliRunner
from wafrunner_cli.main import app

def test_command_success():
    runner = CliRunner()
    result = runner.invoke(app, ["research", "github", "--id", "CVE-2024-1234"])
    assert result.exit_code == 0

docs.title: 3. Command Verification Tests
docs.text:
Ensures all required commands are present (prevents accidental removal).

Location: tests/test_command_verification.py Run: pytest tests/test_command_verification.py

This test is critical - it verifies that no commands were accidentally removed during merges.

docs.title: All Tests
docs.text:
make test
# or
pytest tests/

docs.title: Unit Tests Only
docs.text:
make test-unit
# or
pytest tests/ -m "not integration"

docs.title: CLI Tests Only
docs.text:
make test-cli
# or
pytest tests/ -k "command"

docs.title: With Coverage
docs.text: pytest tests/ --cov=wafrunner_cli --cov-report=html

docs.title: Pre-Commit Hooks
docs.text:
Pre-commit hooks automatically run before each commit:

  1. YAML validation
  2. End of file fixing
  3. Trailing whitespace removal
  4. Black formatting (auto-fixes)
  5. Flake8 linting
  6. Command verification (ensures all commands exist)

To set up:

make install-dev

docs.title: Linting
docs.text:
make lint
# or
flake8 wafrunner_cli tests scripts

docs.title: Formatting
docs.text:
make format
# or
black wafrunner_cli tests scripts

docs.title: Check (read-only)
docs.text:
make check
# Runs both lint and format checks without modifying files

docs.title: Command Verification
docs.text:
The command verification ensures all 8 required commands exist:

make verify-commands
# or
./scripts/verify-commands.sh
# or
pytest tests/test_command_verification.py

Required commands:

  • github
  • scrape
  • classify
  • init-graph
  • refine-graph (critical - was accidentally removed before)
  • init-scdef
  • update-source
  • links

docs.title: 1. Test Structure
docs.text:
• Use unittest.TestCase or plain pytest functions
  • Group related tests in classes
  • Mock external dependencies (API calls, file system)

docs.title: 2. CLI Testing
docs.text:
• Always use CliRunner from typer.testing
  • Test both success and failure cases
  • Verify exit codes and output messages
  • Mock API clients to avoid real network calls

docs.title: 3. Test Naming
docs.text:
• Files: test_*.py
  • Functions: test_*
  • Classes: Test*

docs.title: 4. Before Committing
docs.text:
Always run:

make check        # Lint and format check
make test         # All tests
make verify-commands  # Command verification

Or let pre-commit hooks handle it automatically.

docs.title: Continuous Integration
docs.text:
In CI/CD pipelines, run:

# Install dependencies
pip install -e ".[test]"

# Run checks
make check
make verify-commands

# Run tests
pytest tests/ --cov=wafrunner_cli --cov-report=xml

docs.title: Pre-commit hooks not running
docs.text: pre-commit install

docs.title: Tests failing after merge
docs.text:
# Check if commands are missing
make verify-commands

# Check for import errors
python -c "from wafrunner_cli.main import app"

# Run specific test
pytest tests/test_specific.py -v

docs.title: Command not found in tests
docs.text:
# Verify command registration
python -c "from wafrunner_cli.commands.research import app; print([c.name for c in app.registered_commands])"

docs.title: Test Coverage Goals
docs.text:
• Aim for: >80% coverage
  • Critical paths: 100% coverage
  • CLI commands: All commands should have tests
  • Error handling: Test error paths

docs.title: Additional Resources
docs.text:
• pytest documentation
  • typer testing guide
  • Pre-commit hooks

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text: This guide covers the basic commands to get started with wafrunner-cli.

docs.title: Configuring the CLI
docs.text:
Before you can use the tool, you need to set your API token. Run configure with no arguments to be prompted securely:

wafrunner configure

See Configuring the CLI for more options.

docs.title: Updating CVE Lookup Data
docs.text:
The CLI maintains a local database mapping CVE IDs to vulnerability UUIDs. You should update this before working with new vulnerabilities:

wafrunner update

Use --verbose to see which CVEs were added, and --save-to-collection to automatically create a collection from any new CVEs:

wafrunner update --verbose --save-to-collection

docs.title: Creating a Collection
docs.text:
Collections are groups of vulnerabilities you want to work with together. Create one by specifying CVE IDs or vulnerability UUIDs:

wafrunner collection create my-vulns --id CVE-2021-44228 --id CVE-2024-1234

You can also create a collection from a file with one identifier per line:

wafrunner collection create my-vulns --file cve-list.txt

docs.title: Running Research
docs.text:
Once you have a collection (or a single identifier), you can run research tasks against it. A typical workflow:

# Search GitHub for related repositories
wafrunner research github -c my-vulns

# Scrape data sources
wafrunner research scrape -c my-vulns

# Classify scraped data
wafrunner research classify -c my-vulns

# Initialize exploit graphs
wafrunner research init-graph -c my-vulns

# Refine exploit graphs
wafrunner research refine-graph -c my-vulns

All research commands accept either -c <collection> or -i <identifier>.

docs.title: Downloading Artifacts
docs.text:
Download exploit graphs to inspect locally:

wafrunner data get-graph -c my-vulns

This saves each graph as a JSON file in ./exploit-graphs/.

docs.title: Viewing Status
docs.text:
Check the current state of a vulnerability's research:

wafrunner research show -i CVE-2021-44228

View data source links and their statuses:

wafrunner research links -i CVE-2021-44228

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
Every command and subcommand supports --help:

wafrunner --help
wafrunner research --help
wafrunner research init-graph --help

Running a command group without a subcommand also shows help.

docs.title: Collection Commands
docs.text: The collection command group provides tools for managing local collections of vulnerabilities.

docs.title: Creating a New Collection
docs.text: You can create a new collection of vulnerabilities using the create subcommand. This is useful for grouping related vulnerabilities for analysis or reporting.

docs.title: From a List of Identifiers
docs.text:
You can create a collection by providing a list of CVE IDs or vulnerability IDs directly on the command line:

wafrunner collection create my-first-collection --id CVE-2021-44228 --id a1ddadd4-1b9d-4fab-90fe-64c1c763cd58

docs.title: From a File
docs.text:
If you have a text file containing a list of identifiers (one per line), you can use the --file option:

# my_ids.txt
CVE-2021-44228
a1ddadd4-1b9d-4fab-90fe-64c1c763cd58

wafrunner collection create my-second-collection --file my_ids.txt

docs.title: Listing Collections
docs.text:
To see all of your local collections, use the list subcommand:

wafrunner collection list

docs.title: Showing a Collection
docs.text:
To view the contents of a specific collection, use the show subcommand:

wafrunner collection show my-first-collection

docs.title: Deleting a Collection
docs.text:
To remove a collection, use the delete subcommand:

wafrunner collection delete my-first-collection

docs.title: Configuring the CLI
docs.text: The configure command sets up credentials and paths used by wafrunner-cli.

docs.title: Setting Your API Token
docs.text:
Run configure with no arguments to be prompted for your API token:

wafrunner configure

The token is stored locally and used to authenticate all API requests.

docs.title: Setting the Forge Path
docs.text:
If you have the Forge test framework installed locally, point the CLI to it:

wafrunner configure --forge-path /path/to/forge

This is required for wafrunner test commands.

docs.title: Setting the Log Directory
docs.text:
Research commands write detailed JSON logs after each run. By default these go to ~/.wafrunner/logs, but you can change the location:

wafrunner configure --log-dir /path/to/logs

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
Option Description (no options) Prompts for API token --forge-path Path to the Forge repository --log-dir Directory for log files (default: ~/.wafrunner/logs)

You can set multiple options at once:

wafrunner configure --forge-path /opt/forge --log-dir /var/log/wafrunner

When options are provided, the API token prompt is skipped.

docs.title: Data Commands
docs.text: The data command group downloads research artifacts from the wafrunner platform to your local machine. This is useful when you want to inspect, diff, or process exploit graphs and security controls locally rather than viewing them through research show.

docs.title: get-graph — Download Exploit Graphs
docs.text: Downloads the exploit graph for one or more vulnerabilities and saves each as a JSON file on disk.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. Resolves each identifier (CVE ID or UUID) to a vulnerability record
  2. For each vulnerability, calls GET /vulnerability_records/{vulnID}/exploit-graph
  3. If the API returns a graph with at least one exploit vector, saves it as a JSON file
  4. If the graph doesn't exist yet (404) or is empty, reports it as "no graph available" and moves on
  5. If the file already exists locally, skips it (unless --force is set)

For collections, requests run concurrently using a thread pool. The worker count auto-scales based on collection size (2-8 threads) but can be overridden with -t.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Download a single graph:

wafrunner data get-graph -i CVE-2021-44228

Download graphs for an entire collection:

wafrunner data get-graph -c my-collection

Re-download graphs that already exist locally:

wafrunner data get-graph -c my-collection --force

docs.title: Output files
docs.text:
By default, graphs are saved to ./exploit-graphs/ with the CVE ID as the filename:

./exploit-graphs/
  CVE-2021-44228.json
  CVE-2024-1234.json
  CVE-2023-5678.json

Each file contains the full API response including the exploitGraphInstanceID, timestamps, and the exploitGraph array of exploit vectors.

Use --uuid to name files by the vulnerability UUID instead (e.g. a1ddadd4-1b9d-4fab-90fe-64c1c763cd58.json). Use -o to change the output directory.

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
Option Short Description --collection -c Name of a collection to process --id -i A single CVE ID or vulnerability UUID --output-dir -o Where to save files (default: ./exploit-graphs) --force -f Overwrite files that already exist locally --uuid Use vulnerability UUID as filename instead of CVE ID --max-workers -t Thread count (default: 4, auto-adjusted by collection size) --verbose -V Show per-vulnerability progress and errors

You must provide either --collection or --id, but not both.

docs.title: Summary output
docs.text:
After processing, you'll see a breakdown:

--- Download Summary ---
Processed 10/10 vulnerabilities.
Downloaded:          6    # New graphs fetched and saved
Skipped (exists):    2    # Local file already present, use --force to re-download
No graph available:  1    # API returned 404 or empty graph — run init-graph first
Failed:              1    # API error or network failure
Total time: 4.32 seconds (2.31 items/sec)

If any downloads failed, the specific errors are listed below the summary.

docs.title: Typical workflow
docs.text:
# 1. Create a collection
wafrunner collection create log4j-vulns --id CVE-2021-44228 --id CVE-2021-45046

# 2. Initialize exploit graphs for anything that doesn't have one yet
wafrunner research init-graph -c log4j-vulns

# 3. Download the graphs locally
wafrunner data get-graph -c log4j-vulns

# 4. Inspect a graph
cat ./exploit-graphs/CVE-2021-44228.json | jq '.exploitGraph | length'

docs.title: get-schema — Fetch Exploit Graph Schema
docs.text: Downloads the JSON schema that defines the structure of exploit graphs. Useful for validating graph data or understanding the expected format.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Print the schema to stdout (syntax-highlighted):

wafrunner data get-schema

Save the schema to a file:

wafrunner data get-schema -o exploit-graph-schema.json

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Short Description --output -o Save schema to a file instead of printing to stdout

docs.title: get-controls — Download Security Controls
docs.text:
wafrunner data get-controls --vulnid <vuln-id>
wafrunner data get-controls --cve-id <cve-id>

This command is a placeholder and will be implemented in a future release.

docs.title: Research Commands
docs.text: The research command group triggers research and analysis tasks against vulnerabilities. All commands accept either --collection/-c (a collection name) or --id/-i (a single CVE ID or vulnerability UUID), but not both.

docs.title: Common Options
docs.text:
Most research commands share these options:

Option Short Description --collection -c Collection name to process --id -i Single CVE ID or vulnerability UUID --max-workers -t Thread count (default: 4, auto-adjusted by collection size) --verbose -V Show detailed per-vulnerability output --log-dir Custom directory for JSON log files

docs.title: github — Search GitHub
docs.text:
Searches GitHub for public repositories related to each vulnerability. Skips vulnerabilities that already have a completed search unless --force is set.

wafrunner research github -i CVE-2021-44228
wafrunner research github -c my-collection
wafrunner research github -c my-collection --force

Additional option: --force/-f forces a new search even if one is already complete.

docs.title: scrape — Scrape Data Sources
docs.text:
Triggers a scrape of all data source links associated with each vulnerability.

wafrunner research scrape -i CVE-2021-44228
wafrunner research scrape -c my-collection

docs.title: classify — Run Classifier
docs.text:
Triggers the classifier on scraped data for each vulnerability. By default, skips vulnerabilities where classification is already complete.

wafrunner research classify -i CVE-2021-44228
wafrunner research classify -c my-collection

Additional options:

Option Short Description --update -u Re-run even if status is "complete" or "error" --retry -r Re-run only if status is "error"

--update and --retry are mutually exclusive.

docs.title: init-graph — Initialize Exploit Graphs
docs.text:
Creates exploit graphs for vulnerabilities that don't already have one. Checks for an existing graph first and skips if found.

wafrunner research init-graph -i CVE-2021-44228
wafrunner research init-graph -c my-collection

docs.title: refine-graph — Refine Exploit Graphs
docs.text:
Triggers refinement on existing exploit graphs to improve their quality.

wafrunner research refine-graph -i CVE-2021-44228
wafrunner research refine-graph -c my-collection

docs.title: init-scdef — Initialize Security Control Definitions
docs.text:
Creates security control definitions (SCDEFs) for vulnerabilities. Optionally specify which exploit graph to use.

wafrunner research init-scdef -i CVE-2021-44228
wafrunner research init-scdef -i CVE-2021-44228 --graph <graph-id>
wafrunner research init-scdef -c my-collection

Additional option: --graph/-g specifies a graph ID to use (defaults to API-selected graph).

docs.title: update-source — Update from NIST Source
docs.text:
Triggers an update of vulnerability data from the NIST API source.

wafrunner research update-source -i CVE-2024-1234
wafrunner research update-source -c my-collection

docs.title: links — View Data Source Links
docs.text:
Displays a table of data source links and their processing statuses (scraped, classified, analysed, test category) for each vulnerability.

wafrunner research links -i CVE-2021-44228
wafrunner research links -c my-collection

This command does not support --max-workers or --verbose — it processes sequentially and always shows output.

docs.title: show — View Research Status
docs.text:
Displays the current state of exploit graphs, security control definitions, and data source links for each vulnerability. Useful for checking what work has been done.

wafrunner research show -i CVE-2021-44228
wafrunner research show -c my-collection

Shows: - Exploit graph ID, creation/update timestamps, and vector count - SCDEF IDs, timestamps, and associated exploit vector IDs - Data source link table with scrape/classify/analyse statuses

This command does not support --max-workers or --verbose — it processes sequentially and always shows output.

docs.title: Typical Workflow
docs.text:
# 1. Update local CVE database
wafrunner update

# 2. Create a collection
wafrunner collection create my-vulns --id CVE-2021-44228 --id CVE-2024-1234

# 3. Search for related GitHub repos
wafrunner research github -c my-vulns

# 4. Scrape all data sources
wafrunner research scrape -c my-vulns

# 5. Classify scraped content
wafrunner research classify -c my-vulns

# 6. Initialize exploit graphs
wafrunner research init-graph -c my-vulns

# 7. Refine graphs
wafrunner research refine-graph -c my-vulns

# 8. Initialize security control definitions
wafrunner research init-scdef -c my-vulns

# 9. Check progress
wafrunner research show -c my-vulns
